question.text: An investor holds listed equity shares for 15 months and sells them for a long-term capital gain of Rs. 2,00,000. If STT has been paid, what is the approximate tax liability on the capital gain (as per the rates in the study material)?

answer.text: Rs. 9,375 (12.5% on Rs. 75,000, after exemption of Rs. 1,25,000)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For equity shares/units of equity-oriented funds with STT paid, long-term capital gains tax is 12.5% on gains in excess of Rs. 1,25,000. So taxable gain = Rs. 2,00,000 - Rs. 1,25,000 = Rs. 75,000. Tax = 12.5% of Rs. 75,000 = Rs. 9,375. **The Trap:** Applying 12.5% on the entire gain without the Rs. 1,25,000 exemption would give Rs. 25,000, which is incorrect. **Cross-Exam Context:** Capital gains taxation is tested in NISM Series V-A and Series VIII as well. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Rs. 1,25,000 exemption applies only to equity shares/equity-oriented MF units where STT has been paid. Other capital assets do not get this exemption.
